title: Myrcia carinata
taxonomy: Myrcia carinata is classified within the family Myrtaceae. This family comprises a large group of flowering plants commonly known as myrtles. Myrcia carinata is a species within the genus Myrcia, which is native to the tropical regions of the Americas. The Myrtaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, and its members are often found in areas with high levels of rainfall. The classification of Myrcia carinata is further supported by its characteristics, such as its small white flowers and glossy leaves.

intro: Myrcia carinata, also known as Brazil's guava, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Myrcia carinata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Myrcia carinata is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or syrup, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin care.
